title: Defining the quality standards to use and cite PCR primers and probes from literature?
author: Bhatia, Sudhir
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0

steps.instruction: Aim: Polymerase chain reaction (PCR) primers and probes from medical literature are being used in the recent research work and diagnostics along with making the publications. Our earlier analysis is showing that many of these oligos are not in the position to detect the all strains of various pathogens properly leading to questionable publications. Therefore, our aim is to define some standards to use and cite such primers and probes from literature. Methods: we have analyzed around 500 publications from Pubmed and other sources over a period of last 20 years. The in-silico analysis was done many cases. In many cases, the primers and probes are used in the laboratory settings. Results: we have cited many examples showing that many oligos from literature are outdated, hence the authors using them need to provide proof that such outdated primers and probes are still good enough to cover all different pathogen strains and reviewers during the peer reviewing process needs to pay attention to this important issue. In many cases, we have redesigned to update them to be used in our laboratory and in the commercial kits. Conclusion: we are suggesting initial guidelines, how to solve this issue in the near future and to avoid the questionable publications with outdated oligos, which leading to wastage of tax payer´s money. It is essential to pay attention to this important issue to generate high quality results, which can be part of development of new and effective diagnostics, vaccines and therapies.
